I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
会话锁导致并发消息丢弃：连续发送多条消息只处理第一条
## 问题描述

当用户快速连续发送多条消息（如多张图片）时，cc-connect 只处理第一条，其余消息在会话锁定期间被静默丢弃，session 解锁后也不会补处理。

## 日志证据

```
20:45:17.184 downloaded image  size=244513   ← 第1张，成功下载
20:45:17.184 processing message              ← 第1张，进入处理
20:45:17.195 interactive session started     ← session 被锁
20:45:17.389 message received                ← 第2张消息到达
20:45:17.916 downloaded image  size=266337   ← 第2张，成功下载
20:45:18.003 message received                ← 第3张消息到达
20:45:18.629 downloaded image  size=680155   ← 第3张，成功下载
20:45:47.738 turn complete                   ← 第1张处理完毕
（之后无任何 processing message — 第2、3张被丢弃）
```

## 使用场景

报销场景中员工需要一次性发送多张发票/付款截图。当前只能逐张发送并等待机器人回复后再发下一张，体验很差。

## 期望行为

会话锁定期间收到的消息应该排队等待，session 解锁后依次处理，而不是丢弃。

## 环境

- cc-connect 版本：1.2.1
- 平台：feishu（WebSocket 模式）
- Agent：claudecode

</pr_description>

     1	package core
     2	
     3	import (
     4	        "encoding/json"
     5	        "fmt"
     6	        "log/slog"
     7	        "os"
     8	        "path/filepath"
     9	        "sync"
    10	        "time"
    11	)
    12	
    13	// ContinueSession is a sentinel value for AgentSessionID that tells the agent
    14	// to use --continue (resume most recent session) instead of a specific session ID.
    15	const ContinueSession = "__continue__"
    16	
    17	// Session tracks one conversation between a user and the agent.
    18	type Session struct {
    19	        ID             string         `json:"id"`
    20	        Name           string         `json:"name"`
    21	        AgentSessionID string         `json:"agent_session_id"`
    22	        AgentType      string         `json:"agent_type,omitempty"`
    23	        History        []HistoryEntry `json:"history"`
    24	        CreatedAt      time.Time      `json:"created_at"`
    25	        UpdatedAt      time.Time      `json:"updated_at"`
    26	
    27	        mu   sync.Mutex `json:"-"`
    28	        busy bool       `json:"-"`
    29	}
    30	
    31	func (s *Session) TryLock() bool {
    32	        s.mu.Lock()
    33	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
    34	        if s.busy {
    35	                return false
    36	        }
    37	        s.busy = true
    38	        return true
    39	}
    40	
    41	func (s *Session) Unlock() {
    42	        s.unlock(true)
    43	}
    44	
    45	func (s *Session) UnlockWithoutUpdate() {
    46	        s.unlock(false)
    47	}
    48	
    49	func (s *Session) unlock(update bool) {
    50	        s.mu.Lock()
    51	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
    52	        s.busy = false
    53	        if update {
    54	                s.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
    55	        }
    56	}
    57	
    58	func (s *Session) AddHistory(role, content string) {
    59	        s.mu.Lock()
    60	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
    61	        s.History = append(s.History, HistoryEntry{
    62	                Role:      role,
    63	                Content:   content,
    64	                Timestamp: time.Now(),
    65	        })
    66	}
    67	
    68	// SetAgentInfo atomically sets the agent session ID, agent type, and name.
    69	func (s *Session) SetAgentInfo(agentSessionID, agentType, name string) {
    70	        if agentSessionID == ContinueSession {
    71	                agentSessionID = ""
    72	        }
    73	        s.mu.Lock()
    74	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
    75	        s.AgentSessionID = agentSessionID
    76	        s.AgentType = agentType
    77	        s.Name = name
    78	}
    79	
    80	// GetAgentSessionID atomically reads the agent session ID.
    81	func (s *Session) GetAgentSessionID() string {
    82	        s.mu.Lock()
    83	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
    84	        return s.AgentSessionID
    85	}
    86	
    87	// GetName atomically reads the session name.
    88	func (s *Session) GetName() string {
    89	        s.mu.Lock()
    90	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
    91	        return s.Name
    92	}
    93	
    94	func (s *Session) GetUpdatedAt() time.Time {
    95	        s.mu.Lock()
    96	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
    97	        return s.UpdatedAt
    98	}
    99	
   100	// SetAgentSessionID atomically sets the agent session ID and agent type.
   101	// The ContinueSession sentinel is never persisted — it is only used transiently
   102	// when starting an agent (see engine); storing it on disk breaks resume (#255).
   103	func (s *Session) SetAgentSessionID(id, agentType string) {
   104	        if id == ContinueSession {
   105	                return
   106	        }
   107	        s.mu.Lock()
   108	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
   109	        s.AgentSessionID = id
   110	        s.AgentType = agentType
   111	}
   112	
   113	// CompareAndSetAgentSessionID sets the agent session ID only if it is currently
   114	// empty or still holds the erroneous persisted ContinueSession sentinel.
   115	// Returns true if the value was set, false if a real session ID was already stored.
   116	func (s *Session) CompareAndSetAgentSessionID(id, agentType string) bool {
   117	        if id == "" || id == ContinueSession {
   118	                return false
   119	        }
   120	        s.mu.Lock()
   121	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
   122	        if s.AgentSessionID != "" && s.AgentSessionID != ContinueSession {
   123	                return false
   124	        }
   125	        s.AgentSessionID = id
   126	        s.AgentType = agentType
   127	        return true
   128	}
   129	
   130	func (s *Session) stripContinueSessionSentinel() {
   131	        s.mu.Lock()
   132	        if s.AgentSessionID == ContinueSession {
   133	                s.AgentSessionID = ""
   134	        }
   135	        s.mu.Unlock()
   136	}
   137	
   138	func (s *Session) ClearHistory() {
   139	        s.mu.Lock()
   140	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
   141	        s.History = nil
   142	}
   143	
   144	// GetHistory returns the last n entries. If n <= 0, returns all.
   145	func (s *Session) GetHistory(n int) []HistoryEntry {
   146	        s.mu.Lock()
   147	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
   148	        total := len(s.History)
   149	        if n <= 0 || n > total {
   150	                n = total
   151	        }
   152	        out := make([]HistoryEntry, n)
   153	        copy(out, s.History[total-n:])
   154	        return out
   155	}
   156	
   157	// UserMeta stores human-readable display info for a session key.
   158	type UserMeta struct {
   159	        UserName string `json:"user_name,omitempty"`
   160	        ChatName string `json:"chat_name,omitempty"`
   161	}
   162	
   163	// sessionSnapshot is the JSON-serializable state of the SessionManager.
   164	type sessionSnapshot struct {
   165	        Sessions      map[string]*Session  `json:"sessions"`
   166	        ActiveSession map[string]string    `json:"active_session"`
   167	        UserSessions  map[string][]string  `json:"user_sessions"`
   168	        Counter       int64                `json:"counter"`
   169	        SessionNames  map[string]string    `json:"session_names,omitempty"` // agent session ID → custom name
   170	        UserMeta      map[string]*UserMeta `json:"user_meta,omitempty"`     // sessionKey → display info
   171	}
   172	
   173	// SessionManager supports multiple named sessions per user with active-session tracking.
   174	// It can persist state to a JSON file and reload on startup.
   175	type SessionManager struct {
   176	        mu            sync.RWMutex
   177	        sessions      map[string]*Session
   178	        activeSession map[string]string
   179	        userSessions  map[string][]string
   180	        sessionNames  map[string]string    // agent session ID → custom name
   181	        userMeta      map[string]*UserMeta // sessionKey → display info
   182	        counter       int64
   183	        storePath     string // empty = no persistence
   184	}
   185	
   186	func NewSessionManager(storePath string) *SessionManager {
   187	        sm := &SessionManager{
   188	                sessions:      make(map[string]*Session),
   189	                activeSession: make(map[string]string),
   190	                userSessions:  make(map[string][]string),
   191	                sessionNames:  make(map[string]string),
   192	                userMeta:      make(map[string]*UserMeta),
   193	                storePath:     storePath,
   194	        }
   195	        if storePath != "" {
   196	                sm.load()
   197	        }
   198	        return sm
   199	}
   200	
   201	// StorePath returns the file path used for session persistence.
   202	func (sm *SessionManager) StorePath() string {
   203	        return sm.storePath
   204	}
   205	
   206	func (sm *SessionManager) nextID() string {
   207	        sm.counter++
   208	        return fmt.Sprintf("s%d", sm.counter)
   209	}
   210	
   211	func (sm *SessionManager) GetOrCreateActive(userKey string) *Session {
   212	        sm.mu.Lock()
   213	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   214	
   215	        if sid, ok := sm.activeSession[userKey]; ok {
   216	                if s, ok := sm.sessions[sid]; ok {
   217	                        return s
   218	                }
   219	        }
   220	        s := sm.createLocked(userKey, "default")
   221	        sm.saveLocked()
   222	        return s
   223	}
   224	
   225	func (sm *SessionManager) NewSession(userKey, name string) *Session {
   226	        sm.mu.Lock()
   227	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   228	        s := sm.createLocked(userKey, name)
   229	        sm.saveLocked()
   230	        return s
   231	}
   232	
   233	// NewSideSession registers a new session for userKey without changing the active
   234	// session. Used for isolated one-off runs (e.g. cron with session_mode=new_per_run)
   235	// so the user's current chat remains the default target for normal messages.
   236	func (sm *SessionManager) NewSideSession(userKey, name string) *Session {
   237	        sm.mu.Lock()
   238	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   239	        id := sm.nextID()
   240	        now := time.Now()
   241	        s := &Session{
   242	                ID:        id,
   243	                Name:      name,
   244	                CreatedAt: now,
   245	                UpdatedAt: now,
   246	        }
   247	        sm.sessions[id] = s
   248	        sm.userSessions[userKey] = append(sm.userSessions[userKey], id)
   249	        sm.saveLocked()
   250	        return s
   251	}
   252	
   253	func (sm *SessionManager) createLocked(userKey, name string) *Session {
   254	        id := sm.nextID()
   255	        now := time.Now()
   256	        s := &Session{
   257	                ID:        id,
   258	                Name:      name,
   259	                CreatedAt: now,
   260	                UpdatedAt: now,
   261	        }
   262	        sm.sessions[id] = s
   263	        sm.activeSession[userKey] = id
   264	        sm.userSessions[userKey] = append(sm.userSessions[userKey], id)
   265	        return s
   266	}
   267	
   268	func (sm *SessionManager) SwitchSession(userKey, target string) (*Session, error) {
   269	        sm.mu.Lock()
   270	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   271	
   272	        for _, sid := range sm.userSessions[userKey] {
   273	                s := sm.sessions[sid]
   274	                if s != nil && (s.ID == target || s.Name == target) {
   275	                        sm.activeSession[userKey] = s.ID
   276	                        sm.saveLocked()
   277	                        return s, nil
   278	                }
   279	        }
   280	        return nil, fmt.Errorf("session %q not found", target)
   281	}
   282	
   283	func (sm *SessionManager) ListSessions(userKey string) []*Session {
   284	        sm.mu.RLock()
   285	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   286	
   287	        ids := sm.userSessions[userKey]
   288	        out := make([]*Session, 0, len(ids))
   289	        for _, sid := range ids {
   290	                if s, ok := sm.sessions[sid]; ok {
   291	                        out = append(out, s)
   292	                }
   293	        }
   294	        return out
   295	}
   296	
   297	func (sm *SessionManager) ActiveSessionID(userKey string) string {
   298	        sm.mu.RLock()
   299	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   300	        return sm.activeSession[userKey]
   301	}
   302	
   303	// SetSessionName sets a custom display name for an agent session.
   304	func (sm *SessionManager) SetSessionName(agentSessionID, name string) {
   305	        sm.mu.Lock()
   306	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   307	        if name == "" {
   308	                delete(sm.sessionNames, agentSessionID)
   309	        } else {
   310	                sm.sessionNames[agentSessionID] = name
   311	        }
   312	        sm.saveLocked()
   313	}
   314	
   315	// GetSessionName returns the custom name for an agent session, or "".
   316	func (sm *SessionManager) GetSessionName(agentSessionID string) string {
   317	        sm.mu.RLock()
   318	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   319	        return sm.sessionNames[agentSessionID]
   320	}
   321	
   322	// UpdateUserMeta updates the human-readable metadata for a session key.
   323	// Only non-empty fields are applied (merge behavior).
   324	func (sm *SessionManager) UpdateUserMeta(sessionKey, userName, chatName string) {
   325	        if userName == "" && chatName == "" {
   326	                return
   327	        }
   328	        sm.mu.Lock()
   329	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   330	        meta, ok := sm.userMeta[sessionKey]
   331	        if !ok {
   332	                meta = &UserMeta{}
   333	                sm.userMeta[sessionKey] = meta
   334	        }
   335	        if userName != "" {
   336	                meta.UserName = userName
   337	        }
   338	        if chatName != "" {
   339	                meta.ChatName = chatName
   340	        }
   341	}
   342	
   343	// GetUserMeta returns a copy of the stored metadata for a session key, or nil.
   344	func (sm *SessionManager) GetUserMeta(sessionKey string) *UserMeta {
   345	        sm.mu.RLock()
   346	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   347	        m := sm.userMeta[sessionKey]
   348	        if m == nil {
   349	                return nil
   350	        }
   351	        cp := *m
   352	        return &cp
   353	}
   354	
   355	// AllSessions returns all sessions across all user keys.
   356	func (sm *SessionManager) AllSessions() []*Session {
   357	        sm.mu.RLock()
   358	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   359	        out := make([]*Session, 0, len(sm.sessions))
   360	        for _, s := range sm.sessions {
   361	                out = append(out, s)
   362	        }
   363	        return out
   364	}
   365	
   366	// SessionKeyMap returns a mapping from session ID to the user key (session_key) it belongs to,
   367	// plus active session IDs for each user key.
   368	func (sm *SessionManager) SessionKeyMap() (idToKey map[string]string, activeIDs map[string]bool) {
   369	        sm.mu.RLock()
   370	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   371	        idToKey = make(map[string]string, len(sm.sessions))
   372	        activeIDs = make(map[string]bool)
   373	        for userKey, ids := range sm.userSessions {
   374	                for _, sid := range ids {
   375	                        idToKey[sid] = userKey
   376	                }
   377	                if aid, ok := sm.activeSession[userKey]; ok {
   378	                        activeIDs[aid] = true
   379	                }
   380	        }
   381	        return
   382	}
   383	
   384	// FindByID looks up a session by its internal ID across all users.
   385	func (sm *SessionManager) FindByID(id string) *Session {
   386	        sm.mu.RLock()
   387	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   388	        return sm.sessions[id]
   389	}
   390	
   391	// DeleteByID removes a session by its internal ID from all tracking structures.
   392	func (sm *SessionManager) DeleteByID(id string) bool {
   393	        sm.mu.Lock()
   394	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   395	        if _, ok := sm.sessions[id]; !ok {
   396	                return false
   397	        }
   398	        sm.deleteByIDLocked(id)
   399	        sm.saveLocked()
   400	        return true
   401	}
   402	
   403	// DeleteByAgentSessionID removes all local sessions mapped to the given
   404	// agent session ID. It returns the number of removed local sessions.
   405	func (sm *SessionManager) DeleteByAgentSessionID(agentSessionID string) int {
   406	        if agentSessionID == "" {
   407	                return 0
   408	        }
   409	
   410	        sm.mu.Lock()
   411	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   412	
   413	        removed := 0
   414	        for id, s := range sm.sessions {
   415	                s.mu.Lock()
   416	                matched := s.AgentSessionID == agentSessionID
   417	                s.mu.Unlock()
   418	                if !matched {
   419	                        continue
   420	                }
   421	                sm.deleteByIDLocked(id)
   422	                removed++
   423	        }
   424	        if removed > 0 {
   425	                sm.saveLocked()
   426	        }
   427	        return removed
   428	}
   429	
   430	func (sm *SessionManager) deleteByIDLocked(id string) {
   431	        delete(sm.sessions, id)
   432	        for userKey, ids := range sm.userSessions {
   433	                for i, sid := range ids {
   434	                        if sid == id {
   435	                                sm.userSessions[userKey] = append(ids[:i], ids[i+1:]...)
   436	                                break
   437	                        }
   438	                }
   439	                if sm.activeSession[userKey] == id {
   440	                        delete(sm.activeSession, userKey)
   441	                }
   442	        }
   443	}
   444	
   445	// Save persists current state to disk. Safe to call from outside (e.g. after message processing).
   446	func (sm *SessionManager) Save() {
   447	        sm.mu.RLock()
   448	        defer sm.mu.RUnlock()
   449	        sm.saveLocked()
   450	}
   451	
   452	func (sm *SessionManager) saveLocked() {
   453	        if sm.storePath == "" {
   454	                return
   455	        }
   456	
   457	        // Build a deep-copy snapshot to avoid racing with concurrent Session mutations.
   458	        snapSessions := make(map[string]*Session, len(sm.sessions))
   459	        for id, s := range sm.sessions {
   460	                s.mu.Lock()
   461	                agentSID := s.AgentSessionID
   462	                if agentSID == ContinueSession {
   463	                        agentSID = ""
   464	                        s.AgentSessionID = ""
   465	                }
   466	                snapSessions[id] = &Session{
   467	                        ID:             s.ID,
   468	                        Name:           s.Name,
   469	                        AgentSessionID: agentSID,
   470	                        AgentType:      s.AgentType,
   471	                        History:        append([]HistoryEntry(nil), s.History...),
   472	                        CreatedAt:      s.CreatedAt,
   473	                        UpdatedAt:      s.UpdatedAt,
   474	                }
   475	                s.mu.Unlock()
   476	        }
   477	
   478	        snap := sessionSnapshot{
   479	                Sessions:      snapSessions,
   480	                ActiveSession: sm.activeSession,
   481	                UserSessions:  sm.userSessions,
   482	                Counter:       sm.counter,
   483	                SessionNames:  sm.sessionNames,
   484	                UserMeta:      sm.userMeta,
   485	        }
   486	        data, err := json.MarshalIndent(snap, "", "  ")
   487	        if err != nil {
   488	                slog.Error("session: failed to marshal", "error", err)
   489	                return
   490	        }
   491	        if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(sm.storePath), 0o755); err != nil {
   492	                slog.Error("session: failed to create dir", "error", err)
   493	                return
   494	        }
   495	        if err := AtomicWriteFile(sm.storePath, data, 0o644); err != nil {
   496	                slog.Error("session: failed to write", "path", sm.storePath, "error", err)
   497	        }
   498	}
   499	
   500	func (sm *SessionManager) load() {
   501	        data, err := os.ReadFile(sm.storePath)
   502	        if err != nil {
   503	                if !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   504	                        slog.Error("session: failed to read", "path", sm.storePath, "error", err)
   505	                }
   506	                return
   507	        }
   508	        var snap sessionSnapshot
   509	        if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &snap); err != nil {
   510	                slog.Error("session: failed to unmarshal", "path", sm.storePath, "error", err)
   511	                return
   512	        }
   513	        sm.sessions = snap.Sessions
   514	        sm.activeSession = snap.ActiveSession
   515	        sm.userSessions = snap.UserSessions
   516	        sm.sessionNames = snap.SessionNames
   517	        sm.userMeta = snap.UserMeta
   518	        sm.counter = snap.Counter
   519	
   520	        if sm.sessions == nil {
   521	                sm.sessions = make(map[string]*Session)
   522	        }
   523	        if sm.activeSession == nil {
   524	                sm.activeSession = make(map[string]string)
   525	        }
   526	        if sm.userSessions == nil {
   527	                sm.userSessions = make(map[string][]string)
   528	        }
   529	        if sm.sessionNames == nil {
   530	                sm.sessionNames = make(map[string]string)
   531	        }
   532	        if sm.userMeta == nil {
   533	                sm.userMeta = make(map[string]*UserMeta)
   534	        }
   535	
   536	        for _, s := range sm.sessions {
   537	                s.stripContinueSessionSentinel()
   538	        }
   539	
   540	        slog.Info("session: loaded from disk", "path", sm.storePath, "sessions", len(sm.sessions))
   541	}
   542	
   543	// InvalidateForAgent clears AgentSessionID on all sessions whose AgentType
   544	// does not match the current agent. This handles the case where the user
   545	// switches agent types (e.g. opencode → pi) and stale session IDs from the
   546	// old agent would cause errors.
   547	func (sm *SessionManager) InvalidateForAgent(agentType string) {
   548	        sm.mu.Lock()
   549	        defer sm.mu.Unlock()
   550	
   551	        invalidated := 0
   552	        for _, s := range sm.sessions {
   553	                s.mu.Lock()
   554	                if s.AgentSessionID != "" && s.AgentType != "" && s.AgentType != agentType {
   555	                        slog.Info("session: invalidating stale agent session",
   556	                                "session", s.ID,
   557	                                "old_agent", s.AgentType,
   558	                                "new_agent", agentType,
   559	                                "old_agent_session_id", s.AgentSessionID,
   560	                        )
   561	                        s.AgentSessionID = ""
   562	                        s.AgentType = agentType
   563	                        invalidated++
   564	                }
   565	                s.mu.Unlock()
   566	        }
   567	        if invalidated > 0 {
   568	                sm.saveLocked()
   569	        }
   570	}
   571

    49	// ErrAttachmentSendDisabled indicates that side-channel image/file delivery is disabled by config.

    51	
    52	// RestartRequest carries info needed to send a post-restart notification.
    53	type RestartRequest struct {
    54	        SessionKey string `json:"session_key"`
    55	        Platform   string `json:"platform"`
    56	}
    57	
    58	// SaveRestartNotify persists restart info so the new process can send
    59	// a "restart successful" message after startup.
    60	func SaveRestartNotify(dataDir string, req RestartRequest) error {
    61	        dir := filepath.Join(dataDir, "run")
    62	        if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
    63	                slog.Warn("SaveRestartNotify: mkdir failed", "dir", dir, "error", err)
    64	        }
    65	        data, _ := json.Marshal(req)
    66	        return os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "restart_notify"), data, 0o644)
    67	}
    68	
    69	// ConsumeRestartNotify reads and deletes the restart notification file.
    70	// Returns nil if no notification is pending.
    71	func ConsumeRestartNotify(dataDir string) *RestartRequest {
    72	        p := filepath.Join(dataDir, "run", "restart_notify")
    73	        data, err := os.ReadFile(p)
    74	        if err != nil {
    75	                return nil
    76	        }
    77	        os.Remove(p)
    78	        var req RestartRequest
    79	        if json.Unmarshal(data, &req) != nil {
    80	                return nil
    81	        }
    82	        return &req
    83	}
    84	
    85	// SendRestartNotification sends a "restart successful" message to the
    86	// platform/session that initiated the restart.
    87	func (e *Engine) SendRestartNotification(platformName, sessionKey string) {
    88	        for _, p := range e.platforms {
    89	                if p.Name() != platformName {
    90	                        continue
    91	                }
    92	                rc, ok := p.(ReplyContextReconstructor)
    93	                if !ok {
    94	                        slog.Debug("restart notify: platform does not support ReconstructReplyCtx", "platform", platformName)
    95	                        return
    96	                }
    97	                rctx, err := rc.ReconstructReplyCtx(sessionKey)
    98	                if err != nil {
    99	                        slog.Debug("restart notify: reconstruct failed", "error", err)
   100	                        return
   101	                }
   102	                text := e.i18n.T(MsgRestartSuccess)
   103	                if CurrentVersion != "" {
   104	                        text += fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", CurrentVersion)
   105	                }
   106	                if err := e.waitOutgoing(p); err != nil {
   107	                        slog.Debug("restart notify: outgoing wait cancelled or limited", "platform", platformName, "error", err)
   108	                        return
   109	                }
   110	                if err := p.Send(e.ctx, rctx, text); err != nil {
   111	                        slog.Debug("restart notify: send failed", "error", err)
   112	                }
   113	                return
   114	        }
   115	}
   116	
   117	// RestartCh is signaled when /restart is invoked. main listens on it
   118	// to perform a graceful shutdown followed by syscall.Exec.
   119	var RestartCh = make(chan RestartRequest, 1)
   120	
   121	// DisplayCfg controls how intermediate messages are surfaced.
   122	// A value of -1 means "use default", 0 means "no truncation".
   123	type DisplayCfg struct {
   124	        ThinkingMessages bool
   125	        ThinkingMaxLen   int // max runes for thinking preview; 0 = no truncation
   126	        ToolMaxLen       int // max runes for tool use preview; 0 = no truncation
   127	        ToolMessages     bool
   128	}

   130	// RateLimitCfg controls per-session message rate limiting.
   131	type RateLimitCfg struct {
   132	        MaxMessages int           // max messages per window; 0 = disabled
   133	        Window      time.Duration // sliding window size
   134	}

   136	// Engine routes messages between platforms and the agent for a single project.

   231	// workspaceInitFlow tracks a channel that is being onboarded to a workspace.
   232	type workspaceInitFlow struct {
   233	        state       string // "awaiting_url", "awaiting_confirm"
   234	        repoURL     string
   235	        cloneTo     string
   236	        channelName string
   237	}
   238	
   239	// queuedMessage holds a message that arrived while the session was busy.
   240	// The message is NOT sent to agent stdin at queue time; the event loop
   241	// sends it after the current turn completes to avoid mid-turn interference.
   242	type queuedMessage struct {
   243	        platform      Platform
   244	        replyCtx      any
   245	        content       string
   246	        images        []ImageAttachment
   247	        files         []FileAttachment
   248	        fromVoice     bool
   249	        userID        string
   250	        msgPlatform   string // platform name for sender injection
   251	        msgSessionKey string // session key for extracting chat ID
   252	}
   253	
   254	// interactiveState tracks a running interactive agent session and its permission state.
   255	type interactiveState struct {
   256	        agentSession           AgentSession
   257	        platform               Platform
   258	        replyCtx               any
   259	        workspaceDir           string
   260	        mu                     sync.Mutex
   261	        stopCh                 chan struct{}
   262	        stopped                bool
   263	        pending                *pendingPermission
   264	        pendingMessages        []queuedMessage // messages queued while session was busy
   265	        approveAll             bool            // when true, auto-approve all permission requests for this session
   266	        fromVoice              bool            // true if current turn originated from voice transcription
   267	        sideText               string
   268	        deleteMode             *deleteModeState
   269	        lastAutoCompressAt     time.Time
   270	        lastAutoCompressTokens int
   271	}
   272	
   273	type deleteModeState struct {
   274	        page        int
   275	        selectedIDs map[string]struct{}
   276	        phase       string
   277	        hint        string
   278	        result      string
   279	}
   280	
   281	// pendingPermission represents a permission request waiting for user response.
   282	type pendingPermission struct {
   283	        RequestID       string
   284	        ToolName        string
   285	        ToolInput       map[string]any
   286	        InputPreview    string
   287	        Questions       []UserQuestion // non-nil for AskUserQuestion
   288	        Answers         map[int]string // collected answers keyed by question index
   289	        CurrentQuestion int            // index of the question currently being asked
   290	        Resolved        chan struct{}  // closed when user responds
   291	        resolveOnce     sync.Once
   292	}
   293	
   294	func (s *interactiveState) stopSignal() <-chan struct{} {
   295	        s.mu.Lock()
   296	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
   297	        if s.stopCh == nil {
   298	                s.stopCh = make(chan struct{})
   299	                if s.stopped {
   300	                        close(s.stopCh)
   301	                }
   302	        }
   303	        return s.stopCh
   304	}
   305	
   306	func (s *interactiveState) isStopped() bool {
   307	        s.mu.Lock()
   308	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
   309	        return s.stopped
   310	}
   311	
   312	func (s *interactiveState) markStopped() {
   313	        s.mu.Lock()
   314	        defer s.mu.Unlock()
   315	        if s.stopped {
   316	                return
   317	        }
   318	        s.stopped = true
   319	        if s.stopCh == nil {
   320	                s.stopCh = make(chan struct{})
   321	        }
   322	        close(s.stopCh)
   323	}
   324	
   325	// resolve safely closes the Resolved channel exactly once.
   326	func (pp *pendingPermission) resolve() {
   327	        pp.resolveOnce.Do(func() { close(pp.Resolved) })
   328	}
   329	
   330	func NewEngine(name string, ag Agent, platforms []Platform, sessionStorePath string, lang Language) *Engine {
   331	        ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
   332	        e := &Engine{
   333	                name:                  name,
   334	                agent:                 ag,
   335	                platforms:             platforms,
   336	                sessions:              NewSessionManager(sessionStorePath),
   337	                ctx:                   ctx,
   338	                cancel:                cancel,
   339	                i18n:                  NewI18n(lang),
   340	                attachmentSendEnabled: true,
   341	                display:               DisplayCfg{ThinkingMessages: true, ThinkingMaxLen: defaultThinkingMaxLen, ToolMaxLen: defaultToolMaxLen, ToolMessages: true},
   342	                commands:              NewCommandRegistry(),
   343	                skills:                NewSkillRegistry(),
   344	                aliases:               make(map[string]string),
   345	                interactiveStates:     make(map[string]*interactiveState),
   346	                platformReady:         make(map[Platform]bool),
   347	                startedAt:             time.Now(),
   348	                streamPreview:         DefaultStreamPreviewCfg(),
   349	                references:            DefaultReferenceRenderCfg(),
   350	                eventIdleTimeout:      defaultEventIdleTimeout,
   351	                showContextIndicator:  true,
   352	        }
   353	
   354	        if ag != nil {
   355	                e.sessions.InvalidateForAgent(ag.Name())
   356	        }
   357	
   358	        if cp, ok := ag.(CommandProvider); ok {
   359	                e.commands.SetAgentDirs(cp.CommandDirs())
   360	        }
   361	        if sp, ok := ag.(SkillProvider); ok {
   362	                e.skills.SetDirs(sp.SkillDirs())
   363	        }
   364	
   365	        return e
   366	}
   367	
   368	// SetMultiWorkspace enables multi-workspace mode for the engine.
   369	func (e *Engine) SetMultiWorkspace(baseDir, bindingStorePath string) {
   370	        e.multiWorkspace = true
   371	        e.baseDir = baseDir
   372	        e.workspaceBindings = NewWorkspaceBindingManager(bindingStorePath)
   373	        e.workspacePool = newWorkspacePool(15 * time.Minute)
   374	        e.initFlows = make(map[string]*workspaceInitFlow)
   375	        go e.runIdleReaper()
   376	}
   377	
   378	func (e *Engine) runIdleReaper() {
   379	        ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Minute)
   380	        defer ticker.Stop()
   381	        for {
   382	                select {
   383	                case <-e.ctx.Done():
   384	                        return
   385	                case <-ticker.C:
   386	                        e.reapIdleWorkspaces()
   387	                }
   388	        }
   389	}
   390	
   391	func (e *Engine) reapIdleWorkspaces() {
   392	        if e.workspacePool == nil {
   393	                return
   394	        }
   395	
   396	        reaped := e.workspacePool.ReapIdle()
   397	        if len(reaped) == 0 {
   398	                return
   399	        }
   400	
   401	        reapedSet := make(map[string]struct{}, len(reaped))
   402	        for _, ws := range reaped {
   403	                reapedSet[ws] = struct{}{}
   404	        }
   405	
   406	        type cleanupTarget struct {
   407	                key   string
   408	                state *interactiveState
   409	        }
   410	
   411	        var targets []cleanupTarget
   412	        e.interactiveMu.Lock()
   413	        for key, state := range e.interactiveStates {
   414	                if _, ok := reapedSet[state.workspaceDir]; ok {
   415	                        targets = append(targets, cleanupTarget{key: key, state: state})
   416	                }
   417	        }
   418	        e.interactiveMu.Unlock()
   419	
   420	        for _, target := range targets {
   421	                e.cleanupInteractiveState(target.key, target.state)
   422	        }
   423	        for _, ws := range reaped {
   424	                slog.Info("workspace idle-reaped", "workspace", ws)
   425	        }
   426	}
   427

   452	
   453	// estimateTokens provides a rough token estimate for a set of history entries.
   454	func estimateTokens(entries []HistoryEntry) int {
   455	        return estimateTokensWithPendingAssistant(entries, "")
   456	}
   457	
   458	// estimateTokensWithPendingAssistant is like estimateTokens but includes an assistant
   459	// message not yet written to history (used at EventResult before AddHistory).
   460	func estimateTokensWithPendingAssistant(entries []HistoryEntry, pendingAssistant string) int {
   461	        // Heuristic: ~1 token per 4 characters in mixed English/Chinese.
   462	        count := 0
   463	        for _, h := range entries {
   464	                count += len([]rune(h.Content))
   465	        }
   466	        if pendingAssistant != "" {
   467	                count += len([]rune(pendingAssistant))
   468	        }
   469	        if count == 0 {
   470	                return 0
   471	        }
   472	        return (count + 3) / 4
   473	}
   474	
   475	// SetAutoCompressConfig configures automatic context compression.
   476	func (e *Engine) SetAutoCompressConfig(enabled bool, maxTokens int, minGap time.Duration) {
   477	        e.autoCompressEnabled = enabled
   478	        e.autoCompressMaxTokens = maxTokens
   479	        if minGap <= 0 {
   480	                minGap = 30 * time.Minute
   481	        }
   482	        e.autoCompressMinGap = minGap
   483	}
   484	
   485	// SetResetOnIdle configures automatic session rotation after prolonged inactivity.
   486	// A zero or negative duration disables the behavior.
   487	func (e *Engine) SetResetOnIdle(d time.Duration) {
   488	        if d <= 0 {
   489	                e.resetOnIdle = 0
   490	                return
   491	        }
   492	        e.resetOnIdle = d
   493	}
   494	
   495	// SetShowContextIndicator controls whether assistant replies include the [ctx: ~N%] suffix.
   496	func (e *Engine) SetShowContextIndicator(show bool) {
   497	        e.showContextIndicator = show
   498	}

   500	func (e *Engine) SetWebSetupFunc(fn func() (int, string, bool, error)) { e.webSetupFunc = fn }
   501	func (e *Engine) SetWebStatusFunc(fn func() string)                    { e.webStatusFunc = fn }
   502	
   503	// SetInjectSender controls whether sender identity (platform and user ID) is
   504	// prepended to each message before forwarding it to the agent. When enabled,
   505	// the agent receives a preamble line like:
   506	//
   507	//      [cc-connect sender_id=ou_abc123 platform=feishu]
   508	//
   509	// This allows the agent to identify who sent the message and adjust behavior
   510	// accordingly (e.g. personal task views, role-based access control).
   511	func (e *Engine) SetInjectSender(v bool) {
   512	        e.injectSender = v
   513	}

  1521	func (e *Engine) maybeAutoResetSessionOnIdle(p Platform, msg *Message, sessions *SessionManager, interactiveKey string, session *Session) *Session {
  1522	        if e.resetOnIdle <= 0 || session == nil {
  1523	                return nil
  1524	        }
  1525	
  1526	        hasBackend := session.GetAgentSessionID() != ""
  1527	        hasHistory := len(session.GetHistory(1)) > 0
  1528	        if !hasBackend && !hasHistory {
  1529	                return nil
  1530	        }
  1531	
  1532	        lastActive := session.GetUpdatedAt()
  1533	        if lastActive.IsZero() || time.Since(lastActive) < e.resetOnIdle {
  1534	                return nil
  1535	        }
  1536	
  1537	        slog.Info("auto-resetting idle session",
  1538	                "session_key", msg.SessionKey,
  1539	                "session_id", session.ID,
  1540	                "idle_for", time.Since(lastActive),
  1541	                "threshold", e.resetOnIdle,
  1542	        )
  1543	
  1544	        // Check if the old session has an agent process that needs graceful
  1545	        // shutdown. If so, tell the user we're wrapping up before blocking.
  1546	        e.interactiveMu.Lock()
  1547	        state, hasState := e.interactiveStates[interactiveKey]
  1548	        hasAgent := hasState && state != nil && state.agentSession != nil && state.agentSession.Alive()
  1549	        e.interactiveMu.Unlock()
  1550	
  1551	        if hasAgent {
  1552	                // Notify the user before the potentially long close. The close
  1553	                // returns as soon as the process exits (usually seconds), but
  1554	                // Stop hooks can take up to 120s.
  1555	                e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgSessionClosingGraceful))
  1556	        }
  1557	
  1558	        e.cleanupInteractiveState(interactiveKey)
  1559	        session.UnlockWithoutUpdate()
  1560	
  1561	        newSession := sessions.NewSession(msg.SessionKey, "")
  1562	        if !newSession.TryLock() {
  1563	                slog.Error("failed to lock new session after idle auto-reset", "session_key", msg.SessionKey, "new_session", newSession.ID)
  1564	                return nil
  1565	        }
  1566	
  1567	        e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.Tf(MsgSessionAutoResetIdle, int(e.resetOnIdle/time.Minute)))
  1568	        return newSession
  1569	}
  1570	
  1571	// queueMessageForBusySession queues a message for later delivery when the
  1572	// session is busy. The message is NOT sent to agent stdin at queue time;
  1573	// the event loop sends it after the current turn's EventResult is received.
  1574	// Returns true if the message was successfully queued, false otherwise.
  1575	func (e *Engine) queueMessageForBusySession(p Platform, msg *Message, interactiveKey string) bool {
  1576	        e.interactiveMu.Lock()
  1577	        state, hasState := e.interactiveStates[interactiveKey]
  1578	        e.interactiveMu.Unlock()
  1579	
  1580	        if !hasState || state == nil || state.agentSession == nil || !state.agentSession.Alive() {
  1581	                return false
  1582	        }
  1583	
  1584	        // Only queue metadata — do NOT send to agent stdin yet.
  1585	        // The agent CLI may treat a mid-turn stdin message as part of the
  1586	        // current turn, causing the event loop to hang waiting for a second
  1587	        // EventResult that never arrives. Instead, the event loop sends the
  1588	        // message after the current turn's EventResult is received.
  1589	        state.mu.Lock()
  1590	        if len(state.pendingMessages) >= maxQueuedMessages {
  1591	                state.mu.Unlock()
  1592	                return false // fall back to "previous processing" reply
  1593	        }
  1594	        state.pendingMessages = append(state.pendingMessages, queuedMessage{
  1595	                platform:      p,
  1596	                replyCtx:      msg.ReplyCtx,
  1597	                content:       msg.Content,
  1598	                images:        msg.Images,
  1599	                files:         msg.Files,
  1600	                fromVoice:     msg.FromVoice,
  1601	                userID:        msg.UserID,
  1602	                msgPlatform:   msg.Platform,
  1603	                msgSessionKey: msg.SessionKey,
  1604	        })
  1605	        queueDepth := len(state.pendingMessages)
  1606	        state.mu.Unlock()
  1607	
  1608	        slog.Info("message queued for busy session",
  1609	                "session", msg.SessionKey,
  1610	                "user", msg.UserName,
  1611	                "queue_depth", queueDepth,
  1612	        )
  1613	        e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgMessageQueued))
  1614	        return true
  1615	}
  1616

  1618	// has already exited. It processes all pending messages in the state, similar
  1619	// to the drain loop in processInteractiveMessageWith but as a standalone
  1620	// goroutine.

  1617	// drainOrphanedQueue is called when a message was queued but the drain loop
  1618	// has already exited. It processes all pending messages in the state, similar
  1619	// to the drain loop in processInteractiveMessageWith but as a standalone
  1620	// goroutine.
  1621	func (e *Engine) drainOrphanedQueue(session *Session, sessions *SessionManager, interactiveKey string, agent Agent, workspaceDir string) {
  1622	        unlocked := false
  1623	        defer func() {
  1624	                if !unlocked {
  1625	                        session.Unlock()
  1626	                }
  1627	        }()
  1628	
  1629	        e.interactiveMu.Lock()
  1630	        state, hasState := e.interactiveStates[interactiveKey]
  1631	        e.interactiveMu.Unlock()
  1632	
  1633	        if !hasState || state == nil || state.agentSession == nil || !state.agentSession.Alive() {
  1634	                if hasState && state != nil {
  1635	                        e.notifyDroppedQueuedMessages(state, fmt.Errorf("agent session ended"))
  1636	                }
  1637	                return
  1638	        }
  1639	
  1640	        unlocked = e.drainPendingMessages(state, session, sessions, interactiveKey)
  1641	}
  1642	
  1643	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1644	// Voice message handling
  1645	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1646	
  1647	func (e *Engine) handleVoiceMessage(p Platform, msg *Message) {
  1648	        if !e.speech.Enabled || e.speech.STT == nil {
  1649	                e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgVoiceNotEnabled))
  1650	                return
  1651	        }
  1652	
  1653	        audio := msg.Audio
  1654	        if NeedsConversion(audio.Format) && !HasFFmpeg() {
  1655	                e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgVoiceNoFFmpeg))
  1656	                return
  1657	        }
  1658	
  1659	        slog.Info("transcribing voice message",
  1660	                "platform", msg.Platform, "user", msg.UserName,
  1661	                "format", audio.Format, "size", len(audio.Data),
  1662	        )
  1663	        e.send(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgVoiceTranscribing))
  1664	
  1665	        text, err := TranscribeAudio(e.ctx, e.speech.STT, audio, e.speech.Language)
  1666	        if err != nil {
  1667	                slog.Error("speech transcription failed", "error", err)
  1668	                e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, fmt.Sprintf(e.i18n.T(MsgVoiceTranscribeFailed), err))
  1669	                return
  1670	        }
  1671	
  1672	        text = strings.TrimSpace(text)
  1673	        if text == "" {
  1674	                e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgVoiceEmpty))
  1675	                return
  1676	        }
  1677	
  1678	        slog.Info("voice transcribed", "text_len", len(text))
  1679	        e.send(p, msg.ReplyCtx, fmt.Sprintf(e.i18n.T(MsgVoiceTranscribed), text))
  1680	
  1681	        // Replace audio with transcribed text and re-dispatch
  1682	        msg.Audio = nil
  1683	        msg.Content = text
  1684	        msg.FromVoice = true
  1685	        e.handleMessage(p, msg)
  1686	}

  1893	// processInteractiveMessageWith is the core interactive processing loop.
  1894	// It accepts an explicit agent, interactiveKey (for the interactiveStates map),
  1895	// and workspaceDir so that multi-workspace mode can route to per-workspace agents.
  1896	// ccSessionKey, when non-empty, is used for CC_SESSION_KEY in the agent env; otherwise interactiveKey is used.
  1897	func (e *Engine) processInteractiveMessageWith(p Platform, msg *Message, session *Session, agent Agent, sessions *SessionManager, interactiveKey string, workspaceDir string, ccSessionKey string) {
  1898	        // session.Unlock() is NOT deferred here — it is called explicitly in
  1899	        // the drain loop below while holding state.mu to close the race window
  1900	        // between "queue is empty" and "session unlocked". A deferred fallback
  1901	        // ensures the lock is released on early-return paths.
  1902	        unlocked := false
  1903	        defer func() {
  1904	                if !unlocked {
  1905	                        session.Unlock()
  1906	                }
  1907	        }()
  1908	
  1909	        if e.ctx.Err() != nil {
  1910	                return
  1911	        }
  1912	
  1913	        turnStart := time.Now()
  1914	
  1915	        e.i18n.DetectAndSet(msg.Content)
  1916	        session.AddHistory("user", msg.Content)
  1917	
  1918	        // Use the agent override when available (multi-workspace mode)
  1919	        var agentOverride Agent
  1920	        if agent != e.agent {
  1921	                agentOverride = agent
  1922	        }
  1923	        state := e.getOrCreateInteractiveStateWith(interactiveKey, p, msg.ReplyCtx, session, sessions, agentOverride, ccSessionKey)
  1924	
  1925	        // Set workspaceDir on the state for idle reaper identification
  1926	        if workspaceDir != "" {
  1927	                state.mu.Lock()
  1928	                state.workspaceDir = workspaceDir
  1929	                state.mu.Unlock()
  1930	        }
  1931	
  1932	        // Update reply context for this turn
  1933	        state.mu.Lock()
  1934	        state.platform = p
  1935	        state.replyCtx = msg.ReplyCtx
  1936	        state.mu.Unlock()
  1937	
  1938	        if state.agentSession == nil {
  1939	                e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgFailedToStartAgentSession))
  1940	                return
  1941	        }
  1942	
  1943	        if workspaceDir != "" && e.workspacePool != nil {
  1944	                ws := e.workspacePool.GetOrCreate(workspaceDir)
  1945	                ws.BeginTurn()
  1946	                defer ws.EndTurn()
  1947	        }
  1948	
  1949	        // Apply per-message permission mode override (e.g. cron jobs with mode = "bypassPermissions").
  1950	        // Defer restores only when SetLiveMode succeeds for the override.
  1951	        if msg.ModeOverride != "" {
  1952	                if switcher, ok := state.agentSession.(LiveModeSwitcher); ok {
  1953	                        if switcher.SetLiveMode(msg.ModeOverride) {
  1954	                                defer func() {
  1955	                                        defaultMode := "default"
  1956	                                        if ma, ok := e.agent.(interface{ GetMode() string }); ok {
  1957	                                                if m := ma.GetMode(); m != "" {
  1958	                                                        defaultMode = m
  1959	                                                }
  1960	                                        }
  1961	                                        switcher.SetLiveMode(defaultMode)
  1962	                                }()
  1963	                        }
  1964	                }
  1965	        }
  1966	
  1967	        // Start typing indicator if platform supports it.
  1968	        // Ownership is transferred to processInteractiveEvents which manages
  1969	        // stopping/restarting it across queued message turns.
  1970	        var stopTyping func()
  1971	        if ti, ok := p.(TypingIndicator); ok {
  1972	                stopTyping = ti.StartTyping(e.ctx, msg.ReplyCtx)
  1973	        }
  1974	        defer func() {
  1975	                // Stop typing if ownership was NOT transferred to processInteractiveEvents
  1976	                // (i.e. an early return before that call).
  1977	                if stopTyping != nil {
  1978	                        stopTyping()
  1979	                }
  1980	        }()
  1981	
  1982	        // Drain any stale events left in the channel from a previous turn.
  1983	        // This prevents the next processInteractiveEvents from reading an old
  1984	        // EventResult that was pushed after the previous turn already returned.
  1985	        drainEvents(state.agentSession.Events())
  1986	
  1987	        promptContent := e.buildSenderPrompt(msg.Content, msg.UserID, msg.Platform, msg.SessionKey)
  1988	
  1989	        sendStart := time.Now()
  1990	        state.mu.Lock()
  1991	        state.fromVoice = msg.FromVoice
  1992	        state.sideText = ""
  1993	        state.mu.Unlock()
  1994	
  1995	        // Run Send concurrently with processInteractiveEvents. Some agents block inside
  1996	        // Send until the prompt turn finishes (e.g. ACP session/prompt); they may emit
  1997	        // EventPermissionRequest while blocked — the event loop must run in parallel.
  1998	        sendDone := make(chan error, 1)
  1999	        go func() {
  2000	                sendDone <- state.agentSession.Send(promptContent, msg.Images, msg.Files)
  2001	        }()
  2002	
  2003	        e.processInteractiveEvents(state, session, sessions, interactiveKey, msg.MessageID, turnStart, stopTyping, sendDone, msg.ReplyCtx)
  2004	        if elapsed := time.Since(sendStart); elapsed >= slowAgentSend {
  2005	                slog.Warn("slow agent send", "elapsed", elapsed, "session", msg.SessionKey, "content_len", len(msg.Content))
  2006	        }
  2007	        stopTyping = nil // ownership transferred; prevent defer from double-stopping
  2008	
  2009	        // Guard against a narrow race: a message may have been queued between
  2010	        // processInteractiveEvents observing an empty queue and returning here
  2011	        // (session is still locked, so handleMessage's TryLock fails and routes
  2012	        // the message to queueMessageForBusySession). Drain any such orphans.
  2013	        if e.drainPendingMessages(state, session, sessions, interactiveKey) {
  2014	                unlocked = true
  2015	        }
  2016	}
  2017	
  2018	// getOrCreateWorkspaceAgent returns (or creates) a per-workspace agent and session manager.
  2019	// workspace must be a normalized path (from resolveWorkspace or normalizeWorkspacePath).
  2020	func (e *Engine) getOrCreateWorkspaceAgent(workspace string) (Agent, *SessionManager, error) {
  2021	        ws := e.workspacePool.GetOrCreate(workspace)
  2022	        ws.mu.Lock()
  2023	        defer ws.mu.Unlock()
  2024	
  2025	        if ws.agent != nil {
  2026	                return ws.agent, ws.sessions, nil
  2027	        }
  2028	
  2029	        // Create a new agent instance with this workspace's work_dir
  2030	        opts := make(map[string]any)
  2031	        opts["work_dir"] = workspace
  2032	
  2033	        // Copy model from original agent if possible
  2034	        if ma, ok := e.agent.(interface{ GetModel() string }); ok {
  2035	                if m := ma.GetModel(); m != "" {
  2036	                        opts["model"] = m
  2037	                }
  2038	        }
  2039	        // Copy permission mode
  2040	        if ma, ok := e.agent.(interface{ GetMode() string }); ok {
  2041	                if m := ma.GetMode(); m != "" {
  2042	                        opts["mode"] = m
  2043	                }
  2044	        }
  2045	        // Copy run_as_user (and run_as_env) for OS-level isolation. Without
  2046	        // this, per-workspace agents silently bypass the project-level
  2047	        // run_as_user config because their opts map is freshly constructed
  2048	        // above, not inherited from the project-level opts that main.go
  2049	        // already decorated. See cc-connect#496 and the cc-connect/core/runas.go
  2050	        // preamble for why run_as_user has to survive this copy.

  2913	// notifyDroppedQueuedMessages drains pendingMessages from the state and
  2914	// sends an error notification to each queued message's sender. Called when
  2915	// the event loop exits abnormally (EventError, channel closed) and queued
  2916	// messages can no longer be delivered to the agent.
  2917	func (e *Engine) notifyDroppedQueuedMessages(state *interactiveState, reason error) {
  2918	        state.mu.Lock()
  2919	        remaining := state.pendingMessages
  2920	        state.pendingMessages = nil
  2921	        state.mu.Unlock()
  2922	        for _, q := range remaining {
  2923	                e.send(q.platform, q.replyCtx, fmt.Sprintf(e.i18n.T(MsgError), reason))
  2924	        }
  2925	}
  2926	
  2927	// drainPendingMessages processes all queued messages in the state's pendingMessages
  2928	// queue. It atomically unlocks the session when the queue is empty (while holding
  2929	// state.mu) to close the race window between "queue empty" and "session unlocked".
  2930	// Returns true if the session was unlocked by this call.
  2931	func (e *Engine) drainPendingMessages(state *interactiveState, session *Session, sessions *SessionManager, sessionKey string) bool {
  2932	        for {
  2933	                state.mu.Lock()
  2934	                if len(state.pendingMessages) == 0 {
  2935	                        session.Unlock()
  2936	                        state.mu.Unlock()
  2937	                        return true
  2938	                }
  2939	                queued := state.pendingMessages[0]
  2940	                state.pendingMessages = state.pendingMessages[1:]
  2941	                state.platform = queued.platform
  2942	                state.replyCtx = queued.replyCtx
  2943	                state.fromVoice = queued.fromVoice
  2944	                state.mu.Unlock()
  2945

2026-05-29 18:00:47,006 - INFO - swea-agent-chenhg5__cc-connect-567 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I understand the overall architecture:

1. When a message arrives and the session is busy (TryLock returns false), the code attempts to queue it via `queueMessageForBusySession`
2. The queue is stored in `interactiveState.pendingMessages`
3. After the current turn completes (EventResult), the loop checks for queued messages and processes them
4. `drainPendingMessages` handles the post-turn drain

The issue from the PR description is: messages are being received during session busy period and are being silently dropped. The log shows no "processing queued message" log after the first turn completes.

Let me look at the specific flow more carefully. Let me trace through what happens:

1. Message 1 comes → session.TryLock() succeeds → processing starts
2. Messages 2, 3 come → session.TryLock() fails → `queueMessageForBusySession` is called
3. The queue should hold messages 2 and 3
4. When turn 1 completes → the loop should pick up messages from the queue

But wait! Let me look more carefully at the `handleMessage` function. Let me check if there's an issue with the session key mapping or workspace resolution

Now I see the flow. Let me trace through the problem carefully:

1. First message arrives: `session.TryLock()` succeeds → enters `processInteractiveMessageWith`
2. `processInteractiveMessageWith` calls `getOrCreateInteractiveStateWith` → creates interactive state with `agentSession`, `platform`, `replyCtx`
3. First message's turn runs
4. While running, second/third messages arrive: `session.TryLock()` fails → `queueMessageForBusySession` is called

Now the key question: **is `queueMessageForBusySession` being called correctly?**

Let me look at the `queueMessageForBusySession` again:

```go
func (e *Engine) queueMessageForBusySession(p Platform, msg *Message, interactiveKey string) bool {
    e.interactiveMu.Lock()
    state, hasState := e.interactiveStates[interactiveKey]
    e.interactiveMu.Unlock()

    if !hasState || state == nil || state.agentSession == nil || !state.agentSession.Alive() {
        return false
    }
    ...
}
```

The `interactiveKey` is `msg.SessionKey` in single-workspace mode, or `resolvedWorkspace + ":" + msg.SessionKey` in multi-workspace mode.

Now, what happens when `queueMessageForBusySession` returns false? It falls through to send `MsgPreviousProcessing` to the user. But the issue is: **we only reply to the platform with a message saying "previous processing"... but we don't tell the user that the message is being rejected**. In fact, the PR description says the messages are being "silently dropped" with no processing.

Wait, let me re-read the PR description more carefully:

> When user sends multiple messages rapidly, cc-connect only processes the first one, and the rest are silently dropped during session locking. After session unlocks, they're not processed either.

So the problem is that messages ARE being dropped silently. Let me check what the `MsgPreviousProcessing` message says and whether it's actually being sent.

But wait - the PR says session unlocking doesn't trigger re-processing either. Let me look more carefully at the race condition guard:

```go
if e.queueMessageForBusySession(p, msg, interactiveKey) {
    // Race guard: ...
    if session.TryLock() {
        go e.drainOrphanedQueue(...)
    }
    return
}
e.reply(p, msg.ReplyCtx, e.i18n.T(MsgPreviousProcessing))
return
```

So if `queueMessageForBusySession` returns true, it successfully queued the message. Then the race guard tries to lock the session again - if it succeeds, it means the main loop just finished and unlocked, so we need to drain the queue ourselves.

But wait, there's an important problem here. Look at the flow:

1. First turn (processing message 1) is running in `processInteractiveMessageWith`
2. `processInteractiveMessageWith` calls `getOrCreateInteractiveStateWith` which creates the interactiveState **with the `agentSession` already started**
3. The event loop runs
4. When EventResult comes, it checks for queued messages
5. If there are queued messages, it processes them
6. After processing all queued messages, the loop calls `drainPendingMessages` which processes remaining queued messages
7. When queue is empty, `drainPendingMessages` unlocks the session

Now, the race condition: what if between the time `processInteractiveEvents` returns and `drainPendingMessages` is called (line 2009-2015), a message arrives, gets queued, and then `drainPendingMessages` doesn't find it because it hasn't been queued yet? That's exactly the race described in the comment.

Let me trace through this more carefully:

```go
// line 2003
e.processInteractiveEvents(state, session, sessions, interactiveKey, msg.MessageID, turnStart, stopTyping, sendDone, msg.ReplyCtx)
// At this point, the loop may have already processed some queued messages
// But there could be more...

// line 2009-2015
if e.drainPendingMessages(state, session, sessions, interactiveKey) {
    unlocked = true
}
```

The `processInteractiveEvents` already handles queued messages within its own loop (lines 2773-2848). After returning from `processInteractiveEvents`, there should be no more queued messages unless there was a race.

Now let me think about what could go wrong. Let me look at the case where `processInteractiveEvents` returns but there's a race:

In `processInteractiveEvents`, when `EventResult` comes (line 2773-2848):
- It checks `len(state.pendingMessages) > 0`
- If yes, it dequeues one and continues the loop (to process the next turn)

But what if between the time it checks `len(state.pendingMessages) > 0` finds 0, and the time it calls `return`, a new message arrives and is queued? That would be caught by `drainPendingMessages` at lines 2009-2015.

But there's another scenario: what if `processInteractiveEvents` returns via `EventError` or `channelClosed`? In those cases:
- `EventError`: if agent is alive, it just returns WITHOUT draining pending messages (line 2865-2871)
- `channelClosed`: calls `notifyDroppedQueuedMessages` which drains and notifies

So for `EventError`, pending messages would be silently lost! That's a potential issue.

But the PR description says this happens with "consecutive messages" and the logs show normal turn completion for the first message (not an error). Let me look more carefully at the flow after successful turn completion.

Actually, let me re-read the flow. In `processInteractiveEvents`, after EventResult is received:

1. Lines 2773-2848: Check for queued messages. If found, continue.
2. If no queued messages: return.

Then back in `processInteractiveMessageWith`:
3. Lines 2009-2015: `drainPendingMessages` is called

But wait - `processInteractiveEvents` has its own drain loop! Let me re-read carefully:

```go
// Line 2773-2848
// Check for queued messages — if present, continue the event loop
// for the next turn instead of returning.
state.mu.Lock()
if len(state.pendingMessages) > 0 {
    queued := state.pendingMessages[0]
    state.pendingMessages = state.pendingMessages[1:]
    ...
    continue
}
state.mu.Unlock()
```

So it dequeues ONE message at a time and continues the loop. After processing that queued message, it comes back to the top of the for loop and waits for events again. When the queued message's EventResult comes, it checks for more queued messages, and so on. Eventually when there are no more queued messages, it returns.

Then `drainPendingMessages` at lines 2009-2015 is for catching the race condition.

So the flow seems correct in theory... Let me look for a subtlety. Maybe the issue is about how the interactive state is found.

Let me check `getOrCreateInteractiveStateWith

  2221	                if err != nil {
  2222	                        slog.Error("failed to start interactive session", "error", err, "elapsed", startElapsed)
  2223	                        state = &interactiveState{platform: p, replyCtx: replyCtx}
  2224	                        e.interactiveStates[sessionKey] = state
  2225	                        return state
  2226	                }
  2227	        }
  2228	        if startElapsed >= slowAgentStart {
  2229	                slog.Warn("slow agent session start", "elapsed", startElapsed, "agent", agent.Name(), "session_id", startSessionID)
  2230	        }
  2231	
  2232	        if newID := agentSession.CurrentSessionID(); newID != "" {
  2233	                if session.CompareAndSetAgentSessionID(newID, agent.Name()) {
  2234	                        sessions.Save()
  2235	                }
  2236	        }
  2237	
  2238	        state = &interactiveState{
  2239	                agentSession: agentSession,
  2240	                platform:     p,
  2241	                replyCtx:     replyCtx,
  2242	        }
  2243	        e.interactiveStates[sessionKey] = state
  2244	
  2245	        slog.Info("session spawned", "session_key", sessionKey, "agent_session", session.GetAgentSessionID(), "is_resume", isResume, "elapsed", startElapsed)
  2246	        return state
  2247	}
  2248	
  2249	// cleanupInteractiveState removes the interactive state for the given session key
  2250	// and closes its agent session. When an expected state is provided, cleanup is
  2251	// skipped if the map entry has been replaced by a different state — this prevents
  2252	// a stale goroutine (still running after /new created a fresh Session object and
  2253	// a new turn started on it) from accidentally destroying the replacement state.
  2254	func (e *Engine) cleanupInteractiveState(sessionKey string, expected ...*interactiveState) {
  2255	        e.interactiveMu.Lock()
  2256	        state, ok := e.interactiveStates[sessionKey]
  2257	        if len(expected) > 0 && expected[0] != nil && state != expected[0] {
  2258	                // Another turn has already replaced the state — skip cleanup.
  2259	                e.interactiveMu.Unlock()
  2260	                return
  2261	        }
  2262	        delete(e.interactiveStates, sessionKey)
  2263	        e.interactiveMu.Unlock()
  2264	
  2265	        // Notify senders of any queued messages that will never be processed.
  2266	        if ok && state != nil {
  2267	                state.markStopped()
  2268	                e.notifyDroppedQueuedMessages(state, fmt.Errorf("session reset"))
  2269	        }
  2270	
  2271	        if ok && state != nil && state.agentSession != nil {
  2272	                e.closeAgentSessionWithTimeout(sessionKey, state.agentSession)
  2273	        }
  2274	}
  2275	
  2276	func (e *Engine) closeAgentSessionAsync(sessionKey string, agentSession AgentSession) {
  2277	        if agentSession == nil {
  2278	                return
  2279	        }
  2280	        go e.closeAgentSessionWithTimeout(sessionKey, agentSession)

1. User sends multiple messages quickly
2. First is processed
3. Rest are "silently dropped"

The log evidence shows:
- First message gets "processing message" 
- Second and third get "message received" and "downloaded image" but no "processing message"

The queue should work, unless something prevents it. Let me think about what could go wrong...

Looking at the log:
```
20:45:17.184 downloaded image  size=244513   ← 第1张，成功下载
20:45:17.184 processing message              ← 第1张，进入处理
20:45:17.195 interactive session started     ← session 被锁
20:45:17.389 message received                ← 第2张消息到达
20:45:17.916 downloaded image  size=266337   ← 第2张，成功下载
20:45:18.003 message received                ← 第3张消息到达
20:45:18.629 downloaded image  size=680155   ← 第3张，成功下载
20:45:47.738 turn complete                   ← 第1张处理完毕
```

Notice: between 17.195 (session started) and 17.389 (message 2 received), there's only ~200ms. The first message is already being processed. When message 2 arrives at 17.389, the session should be busy because the session was locked at 17.184 (processing message). So `TryLock` should fail and `queueMessageForBusySession` should be called.

Wait, but the log shows `20:45:17.195 interactive session started`. This is from `getOrCreateInteractiveStateWith`. At this point, the interactive state has been created.

So when message 2 arrives at 17.389, `queueMessageForBusySession` looks up the interactive state. Since the first message already created it, it should be found. The agent session should be alive (just started). So the message should be queued.

Then when the first turn completes at 20:45:47.738, the queue should be drained. But the log says "(之后无任何 processing message — 第2、3张被丢弃)".

This means the queue is not being drained! Let me look at this more carefully...

  2672	                                        cleanResponse += contextIndicator(event.InputTokens)
  2673	                                } else if selfPct > 0 {
  2674	                                        cleanResponse += fmt.Sprintf("\n[ctx: ~%d%%]", selfPct)
  2675	                                }
  2676	                        }
  2677	                        fullResponse = cleanResponse
  2678	
  2679	                        turnDuration := time.Since(turnStart)
  2680	                        slog.Info("turn complete",
  2681	                                "session", session.ID,
  2682	                                "agent_session", session.GetAgentSessionID(),
  2683	                                "msg_id", msgID,
  2684	                                "tools", toolCount,
  2685	                                "response_len", len(fullResponse),
  2686	                                "turn_duration", turnDuration,
  2687	                                "input_tokens", event.InputTokens,
  2688	                                "output_tokens", event.OutputTokens,
  2689	                        )
  2690	
  2691	                        replyStart := time.Now()
  2692	                        normalizedResponse := strings.TrimSpace(fullResponse)
  2693	                        state.mu.Lock()
  2694	                        suppressDuplicate := normalizedResponse != "" && normalizedResponse == state.sideText
  2695	                        state.sideText = ""
  2696	                        state.mu.Unlock()
  2697	
  2698	                        // When tool calls happened and prior text was already surfaced in segments,
  2699	                        // only send the unsent remainder. When tool progress is hidden, tool events don't surface
  2700	                        // side-channel messages and segmentStart stays 0, so keep normal finalize flow.
  2701	                        if toolCount > 0 && segmentStart > 0 {
  2702	                                sp.discard()
  2703	                                if segmentStart < len(textParts) {
  2704	                                        unsent := strings.Join(textParts[segmentStart:], "")
  2705	                                        if unsent != "" {
  2706	                                                for _, chunk := range splitMessage(unsent, maxPlatformMessageLen) {
  2707	                                                        if err := sendWorkspaceWithError(p, replyCtx, chunk); err != nil {
  2708	                                                                return
  2709	                                                        }
  2710	                                                }
  2711	                                        }
  2712	                                }
  2713	                        } else if suppressDuplicate {
  2714	                                sp.discard()
  2715	                                slog.Debug("EventResult: suppressed duplicate side-channel text", "response_len", len(fullResponse))
  2716	                        } else if sp.finish(fullResponse) {
  2717	                                slog.Debug("EventResult: finalized via stream preview", "response_len", len(fullResponse))
  2718	                        } else {
  2719	                                slog.Debug("EventResult: sending via p.Send (preview inactive or failed)", "response_len", len(fullResponse), "chunks", len(splitMessage(fullResponse, maxPlatformMessageLen)))
  2720	                                for _, chunk := range splitMessage(fullResponse, maxPlatformMessageLen) {
  2721	                                        if err := sendWorkspaceWithError(p, replyCtx, chunk); err != nil {
  2722	                                                return
  2723	                                        }
  2724	                                }
  2725	                        }
  2726	
  2727	                        if elapsed := time.Since(replyStart); elapsed >= slowPlatformSend {
  2728	                                slog.Warn("slow final reply send", "platform", p.Name(), "elapsed", elapsed, "response_len", len(fullResponse))
  2729	                        }
  2730	
  2731	                        // TTS: async voice reply if enabled
  2732	                        if e.tts != nil && e.tts.Enabled && e.tts.TTS != nil {
  2733	                                state.mu.Lock()
  2734	                                fromVoice := state.fromVoice
  2735	                                state.mu.Unlock()
  2736	                                mode := e.tts.GetTTSMode()
  2737	                                slog.Debug("tts: checking conditions", "mode", mode, "fromVoice", fromVoice, "will_send", mode == "always" || (mode == "voice_only" && fromVoice))
  2738	                                if mode == "always" || (mode == "voice_only" && fromVoice) {
  2739	                                        go e.sendTTSReply(p, replyCtx, fullResponse)
  2740	                                }
  2741	                        } else {
  2742	                                slog.Debug("tts: not enabled", "tts_nil", e.tts == nil, "enabled", e.tts != nil && e.tts.Enabled, "tts_obj_nil", e.tts == nil || e.tts.TTS == nil)
  2743	                        }
  2744

  2746	                        if triggerAutoCompress {
  2747	                                compressor, ok := e.agent.(ContextCompressor)
  2748	                                if ok && compressor.CompressCommand() != "" {
  2749	                                        if pendingSend != nil {
  2750	                                                if err := <-pendingSend; err != nil {
